title: Thame Cerulian

'''Thame Cerulian''' was a famed [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as a [[Jedi Consular|Consular]] towards the end of the [[Golden Age of the Old Republic]] and trained the famed [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and future [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]].

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Thame Cerulian was brought to the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] as an infant and sorted into the [[Thranta Clan/Legends|Thranta Clan]] as a [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiate]], receiving his early lessons from [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] himself. In approximately [[115 BBY]], Cerulian was given a copy of ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'', a guidebook to life in the Jedi Order. Making several notations in its pages, Cerulian aspired to master the theory of Control abilities, something he believed his contemporaries glossed over. He once watched a recording of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Skwelli]], who wielded six [[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsabers]]. He also believed that he would be a master at the [[Form III/Legends|Soresu]] lightsaber form. Always inquisitive and sometimes playful with his comments, Cerulian boasted that he could challenge and win a competition of [[Smashball/Legends|smashball]] between himself and [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] member, Master [[Omo Bouri/Legends|Omo Bouri]], with the use of the a [[Five-point defense]]. His apprentice [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] later stated in the book that Thame knew little about smashball.<ref name="TJP" />

When Cerulian passed the [[Initiate Trials/Legends|Initiate Trials]] and graduated from the [[Jedi academy]], he was selected by the esteemed Jedi Master [[Unskette]] as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. Proud to wear the [[Jedi apparel/Legends|robes]] of a Padawan alongside his master, Cerulian followed Unskette around the galaxy, practicing to master the third form of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] on the way. During their travels, Cerulian and Unskette encountered three [[chain-sickle]] wielding [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] on [[Filve]], and were barely able to escape alive. Later, the pair wound up on [[Orto/Legends|Orto]] where they encountered a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Unidentified Force-sensitive child|child]] worthy of entering the Order. Because the child's parents refused to turn the infant over, even throwing stones at them, the Jedi referred the case to the High Council. At some point, Unskette and Cerulian evacuated the [[Princess of Quanducial]] down a palace wall using a [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hook]]. The hook broke due to the weight, which was far less than the supposed 550 kilogram breaking point. Cerulian and his master once encountered a herd of [[Jakobeast/Legends|Jakobeast]]s, who created a wave which flattened a forest and flipped over their [[Speeder/Legends|Speeder]]. At some point late in his apprenticeship, after having run into a [[Matukai/Legends|Matukai]] [[Unidentified Matukai|adept]], Cerulian helped to broker peace with the [[Colicoid/Legends|Colicoids]] of [[Colla IV/Legends|Colla IV]]; a feat he thought worthy of counting as part of his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]]. When Cerulian had learned all that he could from Master Unskette, he was set to take the Trials at the Temple in the year [[103 BBY]]. While some of his peers failed to complete the tasks assigned, such as [[Rouggle]] and [[Half-Moonsing]], Cerulian made it through each test and was [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighted]] by Grand Master Yoda. Upon ascending to knighthood Cerulian was presented with a special [[Jedi Credit]], a gift given to all [[Corellian Jedi]]. He stored it in his copy of ''The Jedi Path'' for safe keeping.<ref name="TJP" />

As a Knight, Cerulian became extremely interested in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and the study of ancient [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]]. As a [[Jedi Researcher|researcher]], Cerulian had access to a number of texts and forbidden documents housed within the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]]. Cerulian was eventually made a Master and appointed to sit on the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], giving him access to the [[Holocron Vault/Legends|Holocron Vaults]] of the Archives. Convinced that the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] would eventually return, Cerulian intended to learn everything he could so as to be prepared to face them. In [[89 BBY]], Cerulian was impressed by the Initiate Dooku and chose him as his Padawan. Before they could formally meet and begin their partnership, Cerulian was called away on an important mission and departed the Temple. While he was gone, Dooku and his peer, [[Lorian Nod]] infiltrated Cerulian's [[Jedi Temple dormitory|quarters]] and rifled through his collection of texts on the Sith. Fascinated, Nod was encouraged to break into the Temple's Vaults and steal the [[Dark Holocron]]. Discovering this, the Council expelled Nod for lying and blaming Dooku for the theft. Cerulian returned and the apprenticeship of Dooku began; lasting until around [[82 BBY/Legends|82 BBY]].<ref name="LOTJ" /> Having given Dooku his copy of ''The Jedi Path'', Dooku annotated the text, stating that Cerulian lacked knowledge of the fundamentals of being a Jedi.<ref name="TJP" /> Cerulian later remarked that Dooku was the most gifted Padawan he ever saw.<ref name="Yoda: Dark Rendezvous">''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Thame-Signature.png|thumb|right|Thame's signature]]
Cerulian was confident and sure of himself, as was evident in his comments that he wrote in the Jedi Path book. Cerulian also believed that he could teach much of his own skills to a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], but his own apprentice believed he lacked the fundamentals of training.<ref name="TJP" /> His status as a researcher indicates he had an inquisitive and thoughtful nature. The fact that he was permitted access to Sith Holocrons suggests he was strong-willed to resist the effects of these Dark side artifacts. Despite his more playful and cocky nature, he believed the Jedi had not truly defeated the Sith.

==Powers and abilities==
Upon becoming the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Unskette]], Cerulian focused on the [[Form III/Legends|third form]] of lightsaber combat. Cerulian was also adept at meditation, often meditating in a room in the Reconciliation Quarters.<ref name="TJP" /> Judging by his private library, Cerulian was highly knowledgeable about Galactic history.

==Behind the scenes==
In [[2024]], author [[Sean Stewart]] reflected that Cerulian's reference in ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' was likely something he was told to add by [[Lucasfilm]] or its editorial, explaining that he was not someone personally knowledgeable of the wider story of Dooku.<ref name="ThameReference">{{Twitter|username=SStewartWriter|url=status/1752155225375617524|name=[[Sean Stewart]]|date=2024-01-29|quote=The Yoda/Dooku scene was very much me, but I'm guessing the Thame reference was something Continuity had me add. I wasn't in the brain trust holding the wider story, so that connective tissue usually came from Lucas / editorial|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/iTE0f}}</ref>
